feedback.text: I believe the Digital Fairness Act should consider what has been going on with server-dependent videogames since they've existed. As of today, companies are allowed to render them unplayable. Speaking for my generation, this led to many videogames to dissappear during the 2010s, when I was a child. This doesn't happen to other forms of media such as music, movies, TV series, and so on, but it is allowed and common place in the videogame industry, which has led us to not reexperience our childhood at it's fullest, unlike the past generations. By taking this into consideration, many children nowadays will be benefited with the assurance that the videogames they play will never be gone. As a early signer of the "Stop Destroying Videogames" initiative, I feel imperative to express my feedback, so future generations will be capable of reviving the good old times.
